title: repeat-state declaration merge (#454)
manager: Today, declaring the same state in two blocks is a hard error, which blocks the natural workflow where a state picks up properties from several places — a spread here, a styling block there. v8 makes repeated declarations merge like CSS: saying the same thing twice is harmless, saying two conflicting things is a loud error that names both locations. This is what lets groups, spreads, and per-state styling compose instead of colliding.
cs: Per-state attribute maps union across declaration sites; same-key/same-value is idempotent; same-key/different-value at equal cascade tier is a compile error carrying both source spans; tier order theme < state:{} < kind < group-by-depth < state-specific; dragon: fuzz N declarations and assert order-independence of the merged result.
